benefits: Lastrea splendens, also known as the golden spleenwort or spleenwort fern, is a species of fern with potential health benefits. Research suggests that extracts from Lastrea splendens may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant may also have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections. Additionally, Lastrea splendens has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Lastrea splendens and its effects on human health.

intro: Lastrea splendens, also known as the Golden Fern or Lady Fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional uses in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of respiratory issues, and potential antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea splendens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lastrea splendens is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits.
